options.label: A
options.text: Freezing is an endothermic process where a liquid becomes a solid and absorbs heat

options.label: B
options.text: Freezing is an exothermic process where a liquid becomes a solid and releases heat

options.label: C
options.text: Freezing is when a solid becomes a liquid at high temperature

options.label: D
options.text: Freezing is an endothermic process where particles move faster

explanation: Freezing is exothermic because particles lose kinetic energy and heat is released to the surroundings as the liquid forms a solid structure. Option A is wrong because it says endothermic; Option C is wrong because it describes melting, not freezing; Option D is wrong because particles move slower and it's not endothermic.
